source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 大胆的; 敢于冒险的 (of a person, action, or idea) showing an ability to take risks; confident and courageous: a bold attempt 大胆的尝试

headline.core_cn: 大胆的；敢冒险的；醒目的
headline.core_en: confident and willing to take risks; visually strong and noticeable

familiar_bridge.summary: bold 最熟的是“大胆”；也可指颜色、线条、字体很醒目。

core.main: bold 有两条线索：人、行动或想法敢冒风险；视觉上强烈醒目。GRE 中常见于 bold hypothesis、bold move、bold colors。

senses.label: 大胆的；敢冒险的
senses.text: willing to take risks confidently

senses.label: 醒目的；粗体的
senses.text: visually strong or prominent

usage_groups.group_title: 想法或行动敢于冒风险

examples.sentence: The scientist made a bold claim that challenged decades of accepted theory.
examples.translation: 这位科学家提出了一个大胆主张，挑战了几十年来被接受的理论。
examples.teaching_note: bold claim 强调敢于挑战常规。

examples.sentence: Launching the project during a recession was a bold move.
examples.translation: 在经济衰退期间启动这个项目是一个大胆举动。
examples.teaching_note: bold move 常带风险。

usage_groups.group_title: 视觉效果强烈醒目

examples.sentence: The poster used bold colors to attract attention from across the street.
examples.translation: 海报使用醒目的颜色，从街对面也能吸引注意。
examples.teaching_note: 这里 bold 是视觉上的强烈。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: It was brave of her to speak first.
basic_synonyms.translation: 她第一个发言很勇敢。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: brave 强调面对危险不怕；bold 强调敢于冒险或突破。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The architect proposed a daring new design.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 建筑师提出了一个大胆的新设计。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: daring 与 bold 接近，更突出冒险。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The plan was audacious but carefully argued.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 这个计划很大胆，但论证严密。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: audacious 更正式，可能带赞赏或批评。

antonyms.example_sentence: A timid response would not solve the crisis.
antonyms.translation: 胆怯的回应解决不了危机。
antonyms.difference_note: timid 与 bold 的敢冒险相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The design is subtle rather than bold.
antonyms.translation: 这个设计含蓄，而不是醒目。
antonyms.difference_note: subtle 对应 bold 的视觉强烈。

extension.root_affix: bold 作为常见词记忆即可；核心是 confident / noticeable。

word_family.note: em- 表示“使……”，和下一词直接相关。

related_words.note: 帮助理解 bold 的行动义。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

issues_found: Needed include visual meaning without overloading.

Reviewed usage_tree for natural semantic grouping and student-facing clarity.

issues_found: Needed include visual meaning without overloading.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: vt. 使有胆量; 鼓励; 壮胆 give (someone) the courage or confidence to do something or to behave in a certain way: J was emboldened by the beer. 啤酒壮了我的胆 。 | He smiled, and this emboldened her to ask him for help. 他微微一笑 ， 这使她鼓起勇气向他求助

headline.core_en: to give someone courage or confidence to act

familiar_bridge.summary: embolden = make someone bold：让人更敢说、更敢做。

core.main: embolden 常用于 success、support、silence、victory 等因素让某人或群体更有胆量采取行动，有时带负面意味。

senses.label: 使有胆量；鼓励
senses.text: to make someone more confident to act

usage_groups.group_title: 成功、支持或沉默让人更敢行动
usage_groups.when_to_use: 用于 victory、support、encouragement、lack of punishment 等让人更大胆的原因。

examples.sentence: The early victory emboldened the rebels to demand more concessions.
examples.translation: 早期胜利使反叛者更有胆量要求更多让步。
examples.teaching_note: be emboldened by 表示被某事壮胆。

examples.sentence: The government's silence emboldened companies to ignore the rule.
examples.translation: 政府的沉默让公司更敢无视这条规定。
examples.teaching_note: embolden 可带负面意味：让人更敢做不该做的事。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Her support encouraged him to apply.
basic_synonyms.translation: 她的支持鼓励他去申请。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: encourage 更普通；embolden 更强调胆量增加。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The crisis galvanized citizens into protest.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 危机激励市民走上抗议。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: galvanize 强调激发行动；embolden 强调更有胆量。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The evidence fortified her resolve.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 证据坚定了她的决心。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: fortify 更偏强化信念或防御。

antonyms.example_sentence: Repeated failure discouraged many applicants.
antonyms.translation: 反复失败让许多申请者泄气。
antonyms.difference_note: discourage 与 embolden 的鼓起勇气相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: Threats were used to intimidate witnesses.
antonyms.translation: 威胁被用来恐吓证人。
antonyms.difference_note: intimidate 让人不敢行动。

extension.root_affix: em-/en- 常有“使成为”的作用；bold 是大胆的。embolden 就是“使变大胆”。

related_words.note: 帮助理解 embolden 的语义结果。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: embolden cleanly connects to bold and explains positive/negative courage-increasing contexts.
issues_found: OCR phonetic and definition fragments needed cleanup.
revision_actions: Added emboldened by / embolden to act structures.
Reviewed usage_tree for natural semantic grouping and student-facing clarity.

issues_found: OCR phonetic and definition fragments needed cleanup.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: n. [总称 ] 选民 all the people in a country or area who are entitled to vote in an election: The president appealed directly to the electorate. 总统直接向选民们呼吁 。 | The electorate seems to be swinging against him. 选民们似乎转向反对他

headline.core_en: all the people in an area who are entitled to vote

familiar_bridge.summary: elect 是“选举”；electorate 就是拥有投票权的那一群人。

core.main: electorate 是集合名词，指一个国家、地区或选区中有资格投票的人。常用于 political debate、polls、elections。

senses.label: 全体选民
senses.text: all eligible voters in a country or area

usage_groups.group_title: 政治语境中的投票人群
usage_groups.when_to_use: 用于 election、poll、party、candidate、policy 等讨论政治支持度的语境。

examples.sentence: The candidate tried to appeal to a younger electorate.
examples.translation: 这位候选人试图吸引更年轻的选民群体。
examples.teaching_note: electorate 是整体群体，不是单个 voter。

examples.sentence: Polls suggested that the electorate was divided on the issue.
examples.translation: 民调显示选民在这个问题上意见分裂。
examples.teaching_note: the electorate 可作整体主语。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Voters turned out in large numbers.
basic_synonyms.translation: 选民大量参加投票。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: voters 更日常；electorate 是集合名词、更正式。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The policy appealed to her rural constituency.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 这项政策吸引了她的农村选区选民。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: constituency 可指地理选区或支持群体；electorate 指有投票资格的人。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Only registered electors may vote in the local election.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 只有登记选民可以参加地方选举。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: electors 指个人集合；electorate 是整体。

antonyms.example_sentence: Nonvoters can still influence public debate indirectly.
antonyms.translation: 不投票者仍能间接影响公共讨论。
antonyms.difference_note: nonvoters 不参与投票，与 electorate 的投票资格和参与相对。

extension.root_affix: elect 表示“选择、选举”；-or 是人，elector 是选民；electorate 是选民整体。

related_words.note: 理解 electorate 的日常入口。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: electorate now has clear political collective-noun treatment and useful elect family.
revision_actions: Reviewed usage_tree for natural semantic grouping and student-facing clarity.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: n. 利用机会牟取私利; 投机 the taking of opportunities as and when they arise, regardless of planning or principle: political opportunism 政治投机派生 opportunistic /,potju:'nistik/ adj. 投机的 exploiting chances offered by immediate circumstances without reference to a general plan or moral principle: an opportunistic politician 投机的政客

headline.core_en: taking advantage of opportunities without regard for principle or long-term planning

familiar_bridge.summary: opportunity 是“机会”；opportunism 是只看机会、不看原则的投机。

core.main: opportunism 常用于 politics、business、career、policy criticism，批评某人为了眼前利益而改变立场或利用局势。

senses.label: 机会主义；投机
senses.text: self-serving use of opportunities regardless of principle

usage_groups.group_title: 为了眼前利益利用形势或改变原则
usage_groups.when_to_use: 用于 politician、company、movement、policy、career 等被批评缺乏原则的语境。

examples.sentence: Critics saw the sudden policy shift as political opportunism rather than genuine reform.
examples.translation: 批评者认为这次突然的政策转向是政治投机，而不是真改革。
examples.teaching_note: opportunism 常和 principle 对比。

examples.sentence: The company was accused of opportunism after raising prices during the shortage.
examples.translation: 公司在短缺期间涨价后被指责投机取巧。
examples.teaching_note: 商业语境中强调利用局势牟利。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The decision seemed driven by self-interest.
basic_synonyms.translation: 这个决定似乎由自身利益驱动。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: self-interest 可中性；opportunism 明显贬义。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The leader chose expediency over principle.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 领导人选择了权宜，而不是原则。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: expediency 强调短期方便；opportunism 强调利用机会谋利。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: His careerism made colleagues distrust him.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 他的事业投机让同事不信任他。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: careerism 是个人职业上的机会主义。

antonyms.example_sentence: She acted from principle, not opportunism.
antonyms.translation: 她按原则行事，而不是投机。
antonyms.difference_note: principle 是 opportunism 的核心对照。

antonyms.example_sentence: Integrity requires consistency when opportunities tempt us.
antonyms.translation: 当机会诱惑我们时，正直要求保持一致。
antonyms.difference_note: integrity 强调道德一致性。

extension.root_affix: opportunity 是“机会”；opportunist 是利用机会的人；opportunism 是这种行为方式。

word_family.note: 指利用机会谋私利的人。

related_words.note: 和 opportunistic 构成强对照。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: opportunism now clearly distinguishes opportunity from unprincipled exploitation with political/business examples.
issues_found: Needed avoid treating opportunity itself as bad.
revision_actions: Reviewed usage_tree for natural semantic grouping and student-facing clarity.

issues_found: Needed avoid treating opportunity itself as bad.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: n. [aif]; 问题 a question, especially one addressed to an official or organization: The librarian responded to my query. 图书管理员回答了我的问题 。 v.t. 对 … 表示怀疑; 质疑 ask a question about something, especially in order to express one's doubts about it or to check its validity or accuracy: She queried the teacher's decision. 她质疑老师的决定

headline.core_en: a question, especially a formal request for information; to ask or question

familiar_bridge.summary: query 比 question 更正式，常出现在客服、数据库、官方回复、学术讨论里。

core.main: query 可作名词或动词：名词是正式问题或查询；动词是询问、质疑、查询。技术语境中也指数据库查询。

senses.label: 正式问题；询问
senses.text: a formal question or request for information

senses.label: 质疑；询问
senses.text: to ask about or question something

senses.label: 数据库查询
senses.text: a request for information from a database

usage_groups.group_title: 向机构或系统提出查询

examples.sentence: The office received hundreds of queries about the new visa rules.
examples.translation: 办公室收到了数百个关于新签证规定的询问。
examples.teaching_note: queries 常指提交给机构的问题。

examples.sentence: The database query returned thousands of records.
examples.translation: 这次数据库查询返回了数千条记录。
examples.teaching_note: 技术语境中 query 是“查询请求”。

usage_groups.group_title: 对说法或决定提出质疑
usage_groups.when_to_use: 用于 query a conclusion、query a decision、query whether 等正式讨论。

examples.sentence: Several reviewers queried the assumption that growth would continue indefinitely.
examples.translation: 几位审稿人质疑增长会无限持续这一假设。
examples.teaching_note: 作动词时 query 可表示正式质疑。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The student asked a question after class.
basic_synonyms.translation: 学生课后问了一个问题。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: question 最普通；query 更正式或技术化。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The agency opened an inquiry into the accident.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 该机构对事故展开调查。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: inquiry 可指正式调查；query 多是具体问题或查询。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The program interrogates the database for matching records.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 程序查询数据库以寻找匹配记录。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: interrogate 更强，既可指审问，也可用于技术查询。

antonyms.example_sentence: The report answers the central query.
antonyms.translation: 这份报告回答了核心问题。
antonyms.difference_note: answer 是 query 的回应。

extension.root_affix: query 与 quest/question 都有“寻找、询问”的语义联系。

related_words.note: 帮助理解 query 背后的“寻找答案”意象。

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. (话语等 ) 尖刻的; 辛辣的; 刻薄的 marked by a biting, acrimonious, or cutting quality: a tart disposition 刻薄的本性 | a tart manner 读讽的态度

headline.core_cn: 尖刻的；酸的；酸味小馅饼
headline.core_en: sharply critical in speech; sharp or sour in taste; a small pastry

familiar_bridge.summary: tart 的味觉是“酸、刺激”；放到话语上就是“尖刻、刺人”。

core.main: tart 既可形容味道酸，也可形容语气尖刻。GRE 里更常见的是 tart remark / tart reply 这种评论语气。

senses.label: 话语尖刻的
senses.text: sharp and cutting in tone

senses.label: 酸的；刺激的
senses.text: sharp or sour in taste

senses.label: 水果馅饼
senses.text: a small open pastry

usage_groups.group_title: 话语带刺、语气尖刻

examples.sentence: Her tart reply ended the discussion immediately.
examples.translation: 她尖刻的回答立刻结束了讨论。
examples.teaching_note: tart reply 像酸味一样刺人。

examples.sentence: The review is tart but often accurate in its criticism.
examples.translation: 这篇评论语气尖刻，但批评往往准确。
examples.teaching_note: tart 可用于评价批评文字。

usage_groups.group_title: 食物有明显酸味
usage_groups.when_to_use: 用于 apple、berry、wine、flavor 等味觉。

examples.sentence: The tart apples balanced the sweetness of the dessert.
examples.translation: 酸苹果平衡了甜点的甜味。
examples.teaching_note: 具体味觉义帮助理解抽象义。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: His sharp comment surprised the room.
basic_synonyms.translation: 他尖锐的评论让全场吃惊。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: sharp 更通用；tart 常带酸味和语言刺痛感。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The critic was known for her acerbic reviews.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 这位评论家以尖酸的评论闻名。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: acerbic 与 tart 很接近，更书面。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: His caustic remarks alienated his colleagues.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 他刻薄的话疏远了同事。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: caustic 更强烈，像腐蚀一样伤人。

antonyms.example_sentence: A gentle response would have been more helpful than a tart reply.
antonyms.translation: 温和的回应会比尖刻回答更有帮助。
antonyms.difference_note: gentle 与 tart 的尖刻相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The sauce is sweet, not tart.
antonyms.translation: 这个酱是甜的，不酸。
antonyms.difference_note: sweet 对应味觉义。

extension.root_affix: tart 作为整体词记：从“酸味刺舌”到“话语刺人”。

word_family.note: 名词形式，可指语气或味道。

related_words.note: 同样从味觉引申到语言。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

Needed explain figurative language from taste.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: n. (#ECRE; 48f9E an instance of faking or ambiguity: His response to these charges has been a series of denials and fudges. 他对指控的回应完全是否认和扩塞

headline.core_cn: 含糊处理；敷衍搪塞；篡改；软糖
headline.core_en: to avoid giving a clear answer or to manipulate facts; an evasive or vague answer

familiar_bridge.summary: fudge 在甜食里是软糖；在表达里像把边界揉软：不清楚回答，或把数据说得含糊。

core.main: fudge 常用于 answer、issue、numbers、data，表示故意含糊、回避或把事实处理得不老实。GRE 阅读里要抓住 evasive / ambiguous。

senses.label: 含糊其辞；搪塞
senses.text: to avoid a clear answer

senses.label: 篡改；捏造数据
senses.text: to alter facts or figures dishonestly

senses.label: 软糖
senses.text: a soft sweet

usage_groups.group_title: 回答问题时含糊回避

examples.sentence: When asked about the cost, the minister fudged the answer.
examples.translation: 被问到成本时，部长含糊其辞。
examples.teaching_note: fudge the answer 表示不正面回答。

examples.sentence: His response to the charges was a series of denials and fudges.
examples.translation: 他对指控的回应只是一连串否认和搪塞。
examples.teaching_note: 接近fudge 作名词指含糊搪塞。

usage_groups.group_title: 数据或事实被不诚实地处理

examples.sentence: The company was accused of fudging the numbers to hide its losses.
examples.translation: 公司被指控篡改数字以掩盖亏损。
examples.teaching_note: 这里强调用不诚实的方式处理数字。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The witness evaded the question.
basic_synonyms.translation: 证人回避了这个问题。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: evade 强调躲开；fudge 强调用含糊话糊弄过去。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The official equivocated when asked about responsibility.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 被问到责任时，官员含糊其辞。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: equivocate 更正式，强调语言模棱两可。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The data were manipulated to support the claim.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 数据被操纵以支持该主张。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: manipulate 对应 fudge 数据的义项，更严重。

antonyms.example_sentence: The editor asked the author to clarify the argument.
antonyms.translation: 编辑要求作者澄清论点。
antonyms.difference_note: clarify 与含糊处理相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The company disclosed the full cost of the project.
antonyms.translation: 公司披露了项目的全部成本。
antonyms.difference_note: disclose 与回避隐藏相反。

extension.root_affix: 重点记两个正式义：含糊搪塞、篡改数字。

word_family.meaning_cn: 被含糊处理的；被篡改的

related_words.note: 帮助理解 fudge 的回答方式。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.raw_ocr_text: A44eeL v.i. (contribute to) 导致; 促成 help to cause or bring about: gases that contribute to global warming 造成全球变暖的气体

headline.core_en: to give something; to help cause or bring about a result

familiar_bridge.summary: contribute 最熟的是“贡献”；在 contribute to 中常表示“促成、导致”。

core.main: contribute 有两个高频方向：把钱、时间、观点投入某事；或作为原因之一促成某个结果。

senses.label: 贡献；捐助
senses.text: to give money, effort, ideas, or help

senses.label: 促成；导致
senses.text: to be one factor that helps cause a result

usage_groups.group_title: 投入钱、时间、观点或力量

examples.sentence: Several scientists contributed data to the international study.
examples.translation: 几位科学家为这项国际研究贡献了数据。
examples.teaching_note: contribute 后可直接接贡献的内容。

usage_groups.group_title: 作为原因之一促成结果

examples.sentence: Poor planning contributed to the failure of the project.
examples.translation: 规划不周促成了项目失败。
examples.teaching_note: contribute to 表示原因之一，不一定是唯一原因。

examples.sentence: The report identifies stress as a contributing factor in heart disease.
examples.translation: 报告把压力列为心脏病的一个促成因素。
examples.teaching_note: contributing factor 是学术和医学语境常见表达。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Many residents gave money to the shelter.
basic_synonyms.translation: 许多居民给收容所捐钱。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: give 更普通；contribute 强调为共同目标投入。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The family donated books to the library.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 这家人向图书馆捐赠了书籍。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: donate 偏捐赠；contribute 可捐钱、出力、提出观点。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Clear rules facilitated cooperation.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 清晰规则促进了合作。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: facilitate 强调使过程更顺利；contribute to 是作为因素促成结果。

antonyms.example_sentence: Poor communication hindered progress.
antonyms.translation: 沟通不畅阻碍了进展。
antonyms.difference_note: hinder 与 contribute to success 的方向相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The donor withheld funding until the plan improved.
antonyms.translation: 捐助者在计划改进前暂缓拨款。
antonyms.difference_note: withhold 与 contribute 的给予相反。

extension.root_affix: con- 表示共同；tribute 有给予、分配的线索。contribute 可理解成“把一份给到共同目标中”。

word_family.note: 指提供钱、内容或帮助的人。
正式形容词，常见 contributory factor。

related_words.note: 有给予/献上的语义联系。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 令人痛苦的; 带来心理创伤的 emotionally disturbing or distressing: He was reliving his own traumatic past. 他在头脑中重温着对自己造成精神创伤的往事 。 | His first love affair proved rather traumatic. 他的初恋给他造成了创伤

headline.core_cn: 造成创伤的；令人痛苦难忘的
headline.core_en: emotionally shocking or deeply distressing; related to physical or psychological trauma

familiar_bridge.summary: trauma 是“创伤”；traumatic 指经历强烈到留下心理或身体创伤。

core.main: traumatic 常形容 event、experience、past、injury、memory，重点是冲击很大、留下痛苦影响，不只是 unpleasant。

senses.label: 心理创伤性的
senses.text: emotionally shocking and distressing

senses.label: 外伤性的
senses.text: caused by physical injury

usage_groups.group_title: 经历或记忆造成心理创伤

examples.sentence: He was still trying to understand his traumatic childhood.
examples.translation: 他仍在试图理解自己充满创伤的童年。
examples.teaching_note: traumatic 不只是难过，而是留下深层心理影响。

examples.sentence: Her first love affair proved traumatic rather than romantic.
examples.translation: 她的初恋并不浪漫，反而成了一段痛苦创伤。
examples.teaching_note: 保留强调情感经历的创伤性。

usage_groups.group_title: 医学中指身体外伤相关

examples.sentence: The patient suffered a traumatic brain injury in the accident.
examples.translation: 病人在事故中遭受了创伤性脑损伤。
examples.teaching_note: 医学语境中 traumatic 指外力造成的身体创伤。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The news was distressing for the family.
basic_synonyms.translation: 这则消息让家人痛苦。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: distressing 可短期痛苦；traumatic 更深、更持久。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The survivor gave a harrowing account of the disaster.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 幸存者讲述了灾难中令人痛苦的经历。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: harrowing 强调叙述或经历令人揪心。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The defeat was shattering for the team.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 这次失败对团队是毁灭性打击。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: shattering 强调打碎心理状态。

antonyms.example_sentence: Writing about the event became a healing process.
antonyms.translation: 书写这件事成了一个疗愈过程。
antonyms.difference_note: healing 与 traumatic 的伤害相对。

antonyms.example_sentence: Her words were comforting after the traumatic news.
antonyms.translation: 在创伤性消息之后，她的话令人安慰。
antonyms.difference_note: comforting 减轻痛苦。

extension.root_affix: trauma 是“创伤”；-atic 构成形容词。traumatic 就是“与创伤有关的、造成创伤的”。

related_words.note: 帮助理解 trauma 的身体和心理双重方向。

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. iii; BEI find or identify (a valuable or useful part of something): winnow a mass of statements 对大量的陈述进行筛选 | winnow truth from falsehood 辨别真伪

headline.core_en: to separate what is useful or true from what is not; originally to separate grain from chaff

familiar_bridge.summary: winnow 原本是把谷物和糠分开；抽象用法就是从大量信息里筛出有价值的部分。

core.main: winnow 常用于 statements、evidence、candidates、data、truth from falsehood，强调通过筛选去掉无用或假的东西。

senses.label: 筛选；甄别
senses.text: to select the useful part from a larger mass

senses.label: 扬谷去壳
senses.text: to separate grain from chaff

usage_groups.group_title: 从大量信息或候选项中筛出有价值部分

examples.sentence: The committee needed to winnow hundreds of applications to a shortlist of ten.
examples.translation: 委员会必须从数百份申请中筛出十人的候选名单。
examples.teaching_note: winnow 强调从大量材料中筛选。

examples.sentence: Historians try to winnow truth from legend in the ancient accounts.
examples.translation: 历史学家试图从古代记载中辨别事实和传说。
examples.teaching_note: winnow truth from falsehood/legend 是很有 GRE 感的结构。

usage_groups.group_title: 农业中把谷粒和糠分开

examples.sentence: Farmers once winnowed grain by tossing it into the wind.
examples.translation: 农民过去把谷物扬到风中来去壳。
examples.teaching_note: 具体画面帮助记住抽象的“筛选”。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The editor selected three essays for publication.
basic_synonyms.translation: 编辑选了三篇文章发表。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: select 是一般选择；winnow 强调从大量混杂内容中筛掉无用部分。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Investigators sifted the evidence for clues.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 调查人员筛查证据寻找线索。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: sift 与 winnow 接近，常用于 evidence/data。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The list was culled from thousands of entries.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 这份名单是从数千条中筛选出来的。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: cull 可指挑选，也可指剔除不合格者。

antonyms.example_sentence: The archive accumulated documents faster than scholars could winnow them.
antonyms.translation: 档案积累文件的速度超过了学者筛选的速度。
antonyms.difference_note: accumulate 是增加堆积，winnow 是筛减取精。

antonyms.example_sentence: False claims were mixed with reliable evidence.
antonyms.translation: 虚假说法和可靠证据混在一起。
antonyms.difference_note: mix 与 winnow 的分离筛选相反。

extension.root_affix: 记住具体画面：风把糠吹走，留下谷粒。

related_words.note: grain and chaff 的对比帮助理解 winnow。
近义迁移词。

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. fit exceed: The demand for food outstripped supply. 食品供不应求

headline.core_cn: 超过；胜过；超过……的发展速度
headline.core_en: to exceed or go faster than something in amount, speed, or performance

familiar_bridge.summary: outstrip 可以理解成 outrun / exceed：不只是超过，还常表示增长速度把另一方甩在后面。

core.main: outstrip 常用于 demand outstrips supply、growth outstrips resources、one country outstrips another，强调一方超过另一方的速度或规模。

senses.label: 超过；胜过
senses.text: to exceed in amount, speed, or performance

usage_groups.group_title: 需求、增长或速度超过供给和能力
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 超过；供不应求；发展过快

examples.sentence: Demand for clean water soon outstripped the city's supply.
examples.translation: 对清洁用水的需求很快超过了城市供给。

examples.sentence: The company's growth outstripped its ability to train new managers.
examples.translation: 公司的增长速度超过了它培训新经理的能力。
examples.teaching_note: outstrip 常用于“发展太快，配套跟不上”。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Costs exceeded the original estimate.
basic_synonyms.translation: 成本超过了最初估算。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: exceed 很普通；outstrip 更有“甩开、超过速度”的感觉。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The sequel surpassed the original in complexity.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 续集在复杂度上超过了原作。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: surpass 常用于质量或成就超过。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Wages failed to outpace inflation.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 工资增长没能跑赢通胀。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: outpace 更强调速度；outstrip 也可指数量或能力。

antonyms.example_sentence: Infrastructure lagged behind population growth.
antonyms.translation: 基础设施落后于人口增长。
antonyms.difference_note: lag behind 与 outstrip 的领先超过相反。

extension.root_affix: out- 表示超过、在外；strip 在这里不需硬拆。抓住 outstrip = exceed / leave behind。

related_words.note: 同为 out- 超过类动词。
常用于公司、学生、市场表现比较。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 公平的; 公正的 fair and impartial: an equitable price 公平的价格 | a more equitable distribution of available resources 对现有可用资源的更公正的分配

headline.core_en: fair and impartial, especially in how something is shared or decided

familiar_bridge.summary: equitable 和 equal 有关系，但不等于“完全一样”；它强调分配或处理方式公平合理。

core.main: equitable 常用于 distribution、solution、price、treatment、access，重点是对各方公平、合乎正义。

senses.label: 公平公正的
senses.text: fair and impartial in treatment or distribution

usage_groups.group_title: 资源、待遇或解决方案分配得公平

examples.sentence: The policy aims to ensure equitable access to health care.
examples.translation: 这项政策旨在确保医疗服务的公平可及。
examples.teaching_note: equitable access 强调不同群体都能公平获得。

examples.sentence: Both sides accepted the settlement because it seemed equitable.
examples.translation: 双方都接受了这项和解，因为它看起来公平合理。
examples.teaching_note: equitable solution/settlement 常用于法律和政策语境。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The teacher tried to make a fair decision.
basic_synonyms.translation: 老师尽力作出公平决定。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: fair 最普通；equitable 更正式，常用于制度和分配。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: A judge must remain impartial.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 法官必须保持不偏不倚。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: impartial 强调不偏袒；equitable 强调结果或安排公平。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The protesters demanded a just settlement.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 抗议者要求公正的解决方案。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: just 更有道德和法律正义感。

antonyms.example_sentence: The old system was inequitable to rural students.
antonyms.translation: 旧制度对农村学生不公平。
antonyms.difference_note: inequitable 是直接反义。

antonyms.example_sentence: A biased process cannot produce equitable results.
antonyms.translation: 有偏见的过程不可能产生公平结果。
antonyms.difference_note: biased 强调偏向某一方。

extension.root_affix: equ- 有“相等、平衡”的线索；equitable 不是机械相等，而是公平合理。

word_family.note: 名词形式，常用于教育、法律和社会政策。

related_words.note: 帮助理解词根，但 equitable 更强调公平而非完全一样。

source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. 4@28; 抑止; 阻止 prevent or constrain (an activity or idea): stifle political freedom 扼杀政治自由 | Shortages of basic materials stifled economic grow 太.基本原料的匮乏抑制了经济发展 。 3 044] shackle

headline.core_en: to prevent something from developing or being expressed; to suffocate

familiar_bridge.summary: stifle 可以想成“把声音或空气压住”：既可压住笑声，也可压制想法和发展。

core.main: stifle 常用于 debate、innovation、dissent、growth、laughter，也可指空气闷到让人窒息。重点是把本来要表达或发展的东西压下去。

senses.label: 压制；抑制
senses.text: to prevent expression or development

senses.label: 使窒息；憋住
senses.text: to suffocate or hold back a sound

usage_groups.group_title: 观点、创新或发展被压制

examples.sentence: Strict censorship can stifle public debate.
examples.translation: 严格审查会压制公共辩论。
examples.teaching_note: stifle debate 是政治和学术语境常见搭配。

examples.sentence: The rigid rules stifled creativity in the classroom.
examples.translation: 僵硬规则压制了课堂中的创造力。
examples.teaching_note: 强调发展或表达被压住。

usage_groups.group_title: 声音或呼吸被憋住

examples.sentence: She tried to stifle a laugh during the solemn ceremony.
examples.translation: 在庄严仪式上，她努力憋住笑。
examples.teaching_note: stifle a laugh 是很自然的具体用法。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The regime suppressed opposition newspapers.
basic_synonyms.translation: 该政权压制反对派报纸。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: suppress 更通用，可指政治压制；stifle 更像把表达或发展闷住。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Authorities moved quickly to quash the protest.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 当局迅速采取行动镇压抗议。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: quash 更强烈，常指正式镇压或撤销。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Too much supervision can smother initiative.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 过度监督会扼杀主动性。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: smother 和 stifle 都有“闷住”画面，smother 更像覆盖住。

antonyms.example_sentence: The new policy encouraged experimentation instead of stifling it.
antonyms.translation: 新政策鼓励实验，而不是压制它。
antonyms.difference_note: encourage 与 stifle 创造力相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: Open discussion fosters innovation.
antonyms.translation: 开放讨论促进创新。
antonyms.difference_note: foster 强调让某物发展。

extension.root_affix: 用“闷住、压住”的画面记忆最有效。

related_words.note: 帮助理解 stifle 的身体画面。

source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. (wrest sth. from) 抢; 4; 使挣脱 forcibly pull (something) from a person's grasp: She tried to wrest herself from his grasp. #ik Ath + 244 Ati. | He tried to wrest control of the company from his uncle. iKBMAS BF? FE A he Hl AL.

headline.core_cn: 强夺；费力夺取；扭转取得
headline.core_en: to take or pull something away by force or effort

familiar_bridge.summary: wrest 和 wrestle 有画面联系：像摔跤一样用力把东西抢过来。

core.main: wrest 常用于 wrest power/control/land/meaning from someone/something，强调通过力量、斗争或艰难努力把东西夺过来。

senses.label: 强夺；抢得
senses.text: to take by force or struggle

senses.label: 艰难取得或扭出意义
senses.text: to obtain with effort

usage_groups.group_title: 从别人手中夺取权力、控制或物品

examples.sentence: The rebels tried to wrest control of the city from the army.
examples.translation: 反叛者试图从军队手中夺取城市控制权。

examples.sentence: She wrested the knife from his hand.
examples.translation: 她从他手中夺下了刀。
examples.teaching_note: 具体动作中体现用力抢夺。

usage_groups.group_title: 从困难材料中费力取得意义或结果
usage_groups.when_to_use: 用于 meaning、truth、concession、profit 等需要费力争取的抽象对象。

examples.sentence: The scholar tried to wrest meaning from a fragmentary ancient text.
examples.translation: 这位学者试图从残缺的古文本中艰难解读意义。
examples.teaching_note: 抽象用法仍保留“费力夺取”的画面。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The army took the bridge at dawn.
basic_synonyms.translation: 军队在黎明占领了桥。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: take 很普通；wrest 强调用力、抵抗和争夺。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The troops seized the airport.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 部队夺取了机场。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: seize 强调迅速夺取；wrest 强调对抗中费力夺取。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The interviewer extracted a confession.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 采访者套出了一份承认。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: extract 对应抽象“费力取得”义，但暴力感较弱。

antonyms.example_sentence: They surrendered control rather than having it wrested from them.
antonyms.translation: 他们交出了控制权，而不是被别人夺走。
antonyms.difference_note: surrender 是主动放弃控制。

antonyms.example_sentence: The government yielded power after months of protest.
antonyms.translation: 政府在数月抗议后让出了权力。
antonyms.difference_note: yield 与 wrest 的强夺相对。

extension.root_affix: wrest 与 wrestle 画面相近：扭打、搏斗、费力争夺。

word_family.note: 帮助理解 wrest 的力量感。

related_words.note: wrest 的动作和抽象义都带 struggle。

source.raw_ocr_text: adj， 史诗般的; 英勇的; 壮丽的 of heroic or impressive proportions: epic deeds 英雄业迹

headline.core_en: grand, heroic, or impressive in scale; a long heroic poem or story

familiar_bridge.summary: epic 原本是“史诗”；形容词义就是像史诗一样宏大、漫长、英雄化。

core.main: epic 可指史诗作品，也可形容 journey、struggle、battle、scale、film 等规模巨大或经历漫长艰难。

senses.label: 史诗；叙事长诗
senses.text: a long heroic poem or story

senses.label: 史诗般宏大的
senses.text: grand or impressive in scale

usage_groups.group_title: 故事或作品具有史诗规模

examples.sentence: The film is an epic about migration, war, and survival.
examples.translation: 这部电影是一部关于迁徙、战争与生存的史诗作品。
examples.teaching_note: 作名词或形容词都可指宏大叙事。

usage_groups.group_title: 事件或努力规模巨大、过程艰难

examples.sentence: The rescue required an epic effort from volunteers and doctors.
examples.translation: 这次救援需要志愿者和医生作出艰巨努力。
examples.teaching_note: epic effort 强调规模和难度。

examples.sentence: The climbers survived an epic journey through snow and darkness.
examples.translation: 登山者在冰雪和黑暗中挺过了一段艰难漫长的旅程。
examples.teaching_note: journey 被写得像史诗般漫长艰险。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The ceremony was grand but not excessive.
basic_synonyms.translation: 仪式宏大但不过分。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: grand 强调气势；epic 还带故事性和艰巨过程。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The firefighters made a heroic effort.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 消防员作出了英雄般的努力。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: heroic 强调勇敢；epic 强调宏大规模和叙事感。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The discovery was a monumental achievement.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 这项发现是重大成就。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: monumental 强调重要性和规模。

antonyms.example_sentence: What seemed like an epic crisis was actually a minor delay.
antonyms.translation: 看似重大危机，其实只是小延误。
antonyms.difference_note: minor 与 epic 的宏大相反。

extension.root_affix: epic 来自史诗传统，学习重点是“像长篇英雄故事一样宏大”。

related_words.note: 与 epic 同属宏大叙事语义场。

source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. iB; 透露 (秘密等 ) make known (private or sensitive information): The secret has been divulged to no one. 机密没有向任何人泄露过 。 | ~Newsmen divulged that the President had been considering the idea for some time before making it public yesterday. 记者们透露说 ， 总统在昨天公开这一计划前曾对此作过一番考虑 。 3 L344] disclose

headline.core_en: to make private, secret, or sensitive information known

familiar_bridge.summary: divulge 比 tell 更正式，通常说的是本来不该公开或不想公开的信息。

core.main: divulge 常用于 secret、information、identity、source、details，强调把隐私或敏感信息透露出来。

senses.label: 透露；泄露
senses.text: to reveal private or sensitive information

usage_groups.group_title: 把秘密或敏感信息透露出来

examples.sentence: The journalist refused to divulge the identity of her source.
examples.translation: 记者拒绝透露消息源的身份。
examples.teaching_note: refuse to divulge 常用于保密语境。

examples.sentence: The company would not divulge details of the new technology.
examples.translation: 公司不愿透露新技术的细节。
examples.teaching_note: divulge details 是正式表达。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The report revealed serious errors.
basic_synonyms.translation: 报告揭示了严重错误。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: reveal 更宽泛；divulge 更强调私密或敏感信息。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Officials disclosed the cost of the program.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 官员披露了该项目成本。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: disclose 常用于正式公开信息。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: His expression betrayed his anxiety.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 他的表情泄露了他的焦虑。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: betray 可指无意泄露情绪，也可指背叛。

antonyms.example_sentence: The witness concealed key facts.
antonyms.translation: 证人隐瞒了关键事实。
antonyms.difference_note: conceal 与 divulge 相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The agency withheld the names of the victims.
antonyms.translation: 该机构没有公布受害者姓名。
antonyms.difference_note: withhold 强调不提供信息。

extension.root_affix: divulge 作为整体词学习即可，核心是 reveal confidential information。

related_words.note: 常用于法律、金融和新闻语境。

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 内在的; 固有的; 生来就有的 involved in the constitution or essential character of something: an inherent defect 固有缺陷 | an injherent7ig 凡与生俱来的权利

headline.core_cn: 内在的；固有的；本身具有的
headline.core_en: existing as a natural or essential part of something

familiar_bridge.summary: inherent 可以连到 in：某个性质在事物内部，本来就属于它，不是后来外加的。

core.main: inherent 常用于 risk、problem、flaw、value、tension、limitation，强调某性质属于事物本身结构。

senses.label: 内在固有的
senses.text: built into the essential nature of something

usage_groups.group_title: 风险、缺陷或价值属于事物本身

examples.sentence: There is an inherent risk in any attempt to predict the future.
examples.translation: 任何预测未来的尝试都有内在风险。
examples.teaching_note: inherent risk 指风险来自事情本身。

examples.sentence: The debate reflects an inherent tension between freedom and security.
examples.translation: 这场争论反映了自由与安全之间的内在张力。
examples.teaching_note: inherent tension 是学术写作常见表达。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Curiosity is natural in young children.
basic_synonyms.translation: 好奇心在幼儿身上是自然的。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: natural 更日常；inherent 更强调结构或本质中自带。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Art may have intrinsic value beyond its price.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 艺术可能有超越价格的内在价值。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: intrinsic 与 inherent 很接近，常用于 value/quality。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Some abilities may be innate rather than learned.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 有些能力可能是天生的，而非后天习得。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: innate 多用于生物或人天生特质；inherent 可用于系统和事物。

antonyms.example_sentence: The failure was caused by external pressure, not an inherent flaw.
antonyms.translation: 失败源于外部压力，而不是内在缺陷。
antonyms.difference_note: external 与 inherent 的内在性相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The skill is acquired, not inherent.
antonyms.translation: 这项技能是后天获得的，不是天生固有的。
antonyms.difference_note: acquired 对应非固有的后天获得。

extension.root_affix: in- 表示“在里面”；herent 与 adhere 共享“粘附、附着”的语义线索。inherent 可理解成“附着在本质里”。

word_family.note: 词形相近但语义不同，不要混作同义词。

related_words.note: 最有用的高级近义迁移词。

source.raw_ocr_text: A741 HEL n. 语气; 调子; 气氛 a particular quality or tone that reflects or expresses a mood or attitude: There was a note of scorn in her voice. 她的声音中带着一种茂视 。 vt， 注意; 注意到 notice or pay particular attention to (something): noting his mother's unusual gaiety 注意到母亲不寻常的高兴 vt， 指出; 提出; 说 remark upon (something), typically in order to draw someone's attention to it: We noted earlier the difficulties inherent in this strategy. 我们之前就指出了这一方案的问题 。 | It's worth noting that he gave no reason for his decision. 值得一提的是他没有给出任何理由

headline.core_cn: 语气；调子；气氛；笔记；注意到
headline.core_en: a tone or quality in speech, writing, or atmosphere; also a written record or to notice

familiar_bridge.summary: note 常见是“笔记”；本书重点是“语气、调子”：一句话或场景带出的感觉。

core.main: note 的 GRE 相关义常指 tone：a note of irony、a note of caution、a cheerful note，表示话语或氛围中的某种色彩。

senses.label: 语气；调子；气氛
senses.text: a particular tone, mood, or quality

senses.label: 笔记；记录
senses.text: a written record

senses.label: 注意到
senses.text: to notice or mention

usage_groups.group_title: 话语或文章中带出某种语气

examples.sentence: The article ends on a note of cautious optimism.
examples.translation: 文章以谨慎乐观的语气结尾。
examples.teaching_note: a note of... 表示整体语气中的某种色彩。

examples.sentence: Her speech struck a conciliatory note after weeks of conflict.
examples.translation: 经历数周冲突后，她的演讲呈现出和解的语气。
examples.teaching_note: strike a note 是正式表达。

usage_groups.group_title: 记录或提到某个事实

examples.sentence: The report notes that the sample size was small.
examples.translation: 报告指出样本量较小。
examples.teaching_note: note that 常用于正式写作中“指出”。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The tone of the letter was apologetic.
basic_synonyms.translation: 这封信的语气是道歉式的。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: tone 是最直接的熟词；note 更像某种细微语气色彩。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The essay has an undertone of regret.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 这篇文章带有一层遗憾的潜在语气。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: undertone 更隐含；note 可以更明显。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The poem shifts register in the final stanza.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 诗在最后一节改变了语体。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: register 更偏语言风格层级。

extension.root_affix: note 和 notice/notable/noteworthy 同族，都有“注意、标记”的线索。

related_words.note: 帮助理解 note 的本书重点义。

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 值得注意的; 显著的 worthy of attention or notice; remarkable: a notable exception 值得注意的例外 | play a notable role 起显著的作用

headline.core_cn: 显著的；值得注意的；知名人士
headline.core_en: worthy of attention; remarkable; a well-known person

familiar_bridge.summary: notable 来自 note：值得被记下来、被注意到的，就是 notable。

core.main: notable 常形容 achievement、exception、feature、increase、absence，也可作名词指知名人士。它强调值得注意，不一定是巨大。

senses.label: 显著的；值得注意的
senses.text: worthy of notice or attention

senses.label: 知名人士
senses.text: a well-known or important person

usage_groups.group_title: 变化、特点或例外值得注意

examples.sentence: One notable feature of the study is its unusually large sample size.
examples.translation: 这项研究一个显著特点是样本量异常大。
examples.teaching_note: notable feature 用于指出值得关注的点。

examples.sentence: The city is notable for its network of public libraries.
examples.translation: 这座城市以公共图书馆网络而闻名。
examples.teaching_note: be notable for 表示因某特点而值得注意。

usage_groups.group_title: 社会或领域中的知名人物

examples.sentence: Local notables attended the opening of the museum.
examples.translation: 当地知名人士参加了博物馆开幕式。
examples.teaching_note: 作名词时通常用复数 notables。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The report raises an important question.
basic_synonyms.translation: 报告提出了一个重要问题。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: important 强调重要性；notable 强调值得注意。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The experiment produced remarkable results.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 实验产生了显著结果。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: remarkable 语气更强，常带惊讶。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: A prominent scholar defended the theory.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 一位著名学者为该理论辩护。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: prominent 可指地位高或显眼。

antonyms.example_sentence: The difference between the two samples was negligible.
antonyms.translation: 两个样本之间的差异微不足道。
antonyms.difference_note: negligible 与 notable 的值得注意相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The author remained obscure for decades.
antonyms.translation: 这位作者几十年来默默无闻。
antonyms.difference_note: obscure 对应 notable 的知名义。

extension.root_affix: note 表示注意、记录；-able 表示值得或能够。notable 就是“值得注意的”。

word_family.note: 副词，写作中常用于举例突出重点。

related_words.note: 和 notable 接近，但更直接强调值得记录。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: notable now separates adjective and noun use and differentiates from noteworthy/remarkable.
revision_actions: Built analysis and people usage groups.
Reviewed usage_tree for natural semantic grouping and student-facing clarity.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 值得注意的; 显著的 interesting, significant, or unusual: a noteworthy day 值得注意的一天 | It is noteworthy that the gross product of the country increased. 值得注意的是这个国家的总产值增加了

headline.core_en: important, interesting, or unusual enough to deserve attention

familiar_bridge.summary: noteworthy 字面就是“值得记一笔”：某事足够重要、特别或少见，应该被注意。

core.main: noteworthy 比 notable 更直接强调“值得被记录/指出”。常用于 finding、feature、change、absence、example。

senses.label: 值得注意的
senses.text: deserving attention because important or unusual

usage_groups.group_title: 发现、变化或例子值得被特别指出

examples.sentence: It is noteworthy that the poorest regions improved the fastest.
examples.translation: 值得注意的是，最贫困地区改善得最快。
examples.teaching_note: It is noteworthy that 是正式写作常用句型。

examples.sentence: The study's most noteworthy finding concerns the link between sleep and memory.
examples.translation: 这项研究最值得注意的发现涉及睡眠与记忆之间的关系。
examples.teaching_note: noteworthy finding 比 notable 更像报告中提示重点。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The article raises an interesting problem.
basic_synonyms.translation: 文章提出了一个有意思的问题。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: interesting 更宽泛；noteworthy 更正式，强调值得记录。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The policy produced a significant change.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 这项政策产生了显著变化。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: significant 更强调重要程度或统计显著。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Her recovery was remarkable.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 她的恢复很显著。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: remarkable 语气更强，常带惊讶。

antonyms.example_sentence: The results were unremarkable except for one anomaly.
antonyms.translation: 除一个异常外，结果平平无奇。
antonyms.difference_note: unremarkable 与 noteworthy 相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The error was trivial and did not affect the conclusion.
antonyms.translation: 这个错误微不足道，不影响结论。
antonyms.difference_note: trivial 表示不值得注意。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The town is notable for its old theater.
confusable_words.translation: 这座小镇因老剧院而闻名。
confusable_words.difference_note: notable 可指知名或显著；noteworthy 更强调值得被指出。

extension.root_affix: note + worthy = worth noting，值得记下。

related_words.note: 同样是 -worthy 结构：值得报道。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: noteworthy now has a distinct worth-noting profile rather than duplicating notable.
issues_found: Needed provide It is noteworthy that structure.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 无数的; 数不清的 too many to be counted (often used hyperbolically): innumerable stars FRE JR | an innumerable crowd of people 人山人海

headline.core_en: too many to be counted

familiar_bridge.summary: number 是“数字、数量”；innumerable 就是多到无法用数字数清。

core.main: innumerable 用于强调数量非常多，常带夸张色彩，修饰 examples、problems、stars、details、occasions 等复数名词。

senses.label: 无数的；数不清的
senses.text: too many to count

usage_groups.group_title: 数量多到无法一一列举

examples.sentence: The theory has been tested in innumerable experiments.
examples.translation: 这个理论已经在无数实验中得到检验。
examples.teaching_note: innumerable 强调数量多到无法逐一计数。

examples.sentence: The archive contains innumerable letters from ordinary citizens.
examples.translation: 档案馆保存着无数普通公民的信件。
examples.teaching_note: 常用于正式写作中的数量夸张。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Countless people crossed the river each year.
basic_synonyms.translation: 每年有无数人过这条河。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: countless 更日常；innumerable 更正式。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The policy created myriad complications.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 这项政策造成了无数复杂问题。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: myriad 更文学或正式。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The sky seemed filled with numberless stars.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 天空似乎布满数不清的星星。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: numberless 更书面，使用频率较低。

antonyms.example_sentence: Only a few objections were raised.
antonyms.translation: 只提出了少数反对意见。
antonyms.difference_note: few 与 innumerable 的数量感相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The project had limited resources.
antonyms.translation: 项目资源有限。
antonyms.difference_note: limited 强调数量或范围受限。

extension.root_affix: in- 表示否定；numer- 和 number/numerical 有关；-able 表示能够。innumerable 就是“不能被数清的”。

related_words.note: e- + numer，表示把项目一个个数出来。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: innumerable was reviewed with clear numer-root links and natural quantity examples.
issues_found: Needed avoid only glossing as many.
revision_actions: Added one focused quantity usage group.
Reviewed usage_tree for natural semantic grouping and student-facing clarity.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj， 瞬间的; 即刻的 occurring or done in an instant or instantly: an instantaneous response 瞬时的反应

headline.core_en: happening immediately or in an instant

familiar_bridge.summary: instant 是“一瞬间”；instantaneous 形容事情几乎立刻发生，没有明显延迟。

core.main: instantaneous 常形容 reaction、response、change、communication、effect，强调时间上几乎为零。

senses.label: 瞬间的；即刻的
senses.text: occurring or done immediately

usage_groups.group_title: 反应、变化或效果几乎立刻发生

examples.sentence: The drug produced an almost instantaneous reduction in pain.
examples.translation: 这种药几乎立刻减轻了疼痛。
examples.teaching_note: almost instantaneous 常用于科学或医学语境。

examples.sentence: Online messages create the expectation of instantaneous communication.
examples.translation: 在线消息让人们期待即时沟通。
examples.teaching_note: instantaneous communication 强调几乎没有延迟。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The policy had an immediate effect.
basic_synonyms.translation: 这项政策产生了立即效果。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: immediate 最常用；instantaneous 更强调在一瞬间完成。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The app offers instant translation.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 这个应用提供即时翻译。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: instant 更常见，可作名词或形容词。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The two announcements were simultaneous.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 两个公告同时发布。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: simultaneous 强调同时，不一定快；instantaneous 强调没有延迟。

antonyms.example_sentence: The change was gradual rather than instantaneous.
antonyms.translation: 变化是逐渐发生的，而不是瞬间发生的。
antonyms.difference_note: gradual 与 instantaneous 的时间速度相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The delayed response created confusion.
antonyms.translation: 延迟回应造成了困惑。
antonyms.difference_note: delayed 强调有等待时间。

extension.root_affix: instant 是“一瞬间”；-aneous 形成形容词。instantaneous 就是“发生在一瞬间的”。

related_words.note: momentary 强调持续时间短；instantaneous 强调发生得快。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: instantaneous now distinguishes immediate/instant/simultaneous and has clear time-process examples.
revision_actions: Reviewed usage_tree for natural semantic grouping and student-facing clarity.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: v.i. (由于腐朽而 ) 碎裂; 崩塌;腐烂 slowly decay or disintegrate, especially because of neglect: the moldering ruins of an old castle 古堡废地的遗迹

headline.core_en: to decay slowly, especially through neglect

familiar_bridge.summary: molder 可以连到 mold/mould：东西被遗忘、受潮、无人照料后慢慢腐坏。

core.main: molder 常形容 buildings、books、documents、bodies、memories 等在时间和忽视中逐渐腐坏或消逝。

senses.label: 腐烂；朽坏
senses.text: to decay slowly

senses.label: 被遗忘而衰败
senses.text: to decline through neglect

usage_groups.group_title: 物品或建筑因时间和忽视而腐坏

examples.sentence: Old manuscripts were left to molder in a damp basement.
examples.translation: 旧手稿被丢在潮湿地下室里慢慢腐坏。
examples.teaching_note: molder 常带无人照料、慢慢腐败的画面。

usage_groups.group_title: 记忆、制度或影响逐渐衰败消失
usage_groups.when_to_use: 用于 memory、tradition、institution、reputation 等抽象对象。

examples.sentence: Without public support, the old institution began to molder.
examples.translation: 没有公众支持，这个老机构开始衰败。
examples.teaching_note: 抽象用法保留“无人照料而坏掉”的意象。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The wooden beams began to decay.
basic_synonyms.translation: 木梁开始腐烂。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: decay 最普通；molder 更强调慢慢腐坏和被遗忘。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The road deteriorated after years without repair.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 道路多年未维修后恶化。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: deteriorate 更宽泛，可指质量下降。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Organic matter decomposes in the soil.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 有机物在土壤中分解。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: decompose 偏科学分解；molder 更文学。

antonyms.example_sentence: The museum preserved documents that might otherwise molder.
antonyms.translation: 博物馆保存了本可能腐坏的文件。
antonyms.difference_note: preserve 与 molder 的任其腐坏相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: Volunteers restored the moldering chapel.
antonyms.translation: 志愿者修复了那座朽坏的小教堂。
antonyms.difference_note: restore 表示把衰败对象修好。

extension.root_affix: molder 与 mold/mould 的腐坏意象有关；重点记 slow decay through neglect。

related_words.note: 更口语、直接。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: n. 没有文化修养的人; 反感文化艺术的人 a person who does not like or understand art, literature, music, etc.: They're just a bunch of philistines. 他们是一群没文化的人

headline.core_cn: 不懂艺术文化的人；庸俗无文的人
headline.core_en: a person who is indifferent or hostile to art, culture, or intellectual values

familiar_bridge.summary: philistine 不是普通“不懂”，而是对艺术、文学、思想没有兴趣甚至轻视。

core.main: philistine 常用于文化批评，指只重实际、金钱或娱乐而轻视艺术和精神生活的人。作形容词也可表示庸俗、缺乏文化品味。

senses.label: 文化庸人；不懂艺术的人
senses.text: someone indifferent to culture and art

senses.label: 庸俗无文化的
senses.text: uncultured or anti-intellectual

usage_groups.group_title: 轻视艺术和思想的人
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 文化庸人；反感文化艺术的人
usage_groups.when_to_use: 用于 critic、public、administrator、businessman 等被批评缺少文化品味。

examples.sentence: The critic dismissed the mayor as a philistine who cared only about ticket sales.
examples.translation: 评论家把市长斥为只关心票房的文化庸人。
examples.teaching_note: philistine 常用于文化批评，语气很重。

examples.sentence: His philistine attitude toward literature annoyed the teachers.
examples.translation: 他对文学的庸俗态度让老师们很恼火。
examples.teaching_note: 作形容词时表示缺乏文化品味。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: He sounded like an uncultured person when he mocked every painting in the gallery.
basic_synonyms.translation: 他嘲笑画廊里每一幅画时，听起来像个没有文化修养的人。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: uncultured person 是解释性说法；philistine 更尖锐。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The guest behaved like a boor at the concert.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 这位客人在音乐会上表现得像个粗俗无礼的人。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: boor 强调粗鲁；philistine 强调缺乏文化品味。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The movement had an anti-intellectual tone.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 这场运动带有反智色彩。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: anti-intellectual 更直接指轻视思想知识。

antonyms.example_sentence: A connoisseur noticed details that a philistine would ignore.
antonyms.translation: 鉴赏家注意到文化庸人会忽视的细节。
antonyms.difference_note: connoisseur 是懂艺术品味的人。

antonyms.example_sentence: The aesthete valued beauty above utility.
antonyms.translation: 这位审美家把美置于实用之上。
antonyms.difference_note: aesthete 与 philistine 的缺乏审美相对。

extension.root_affix: Philistine 原是历史民族名称，现代小写 philistine 表示缺乏文化品味的人。学生记现代义即可。

related_words.note: 理解 philistine 的核心对照。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: philistine now captures cultural anti-intellectualism and avoids shallow 'uncultured' only gloss.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: phr. 尽管 without being affected by the particular factor mentioned: Jn spite of his age, he still leads an active life. 尽管年事已高 ， 他仍过着忙碌的生活

headline.core_en: despite; without being prevented by something

familiar_bridge.summary: in spite of = despite：后面接名词、代词或动名词，表示“虽然有这个阻碍，结果仍发生”。

core.main: in spite of 是让步结构，不接完整句子；后面通常接 the rain、his age、being tired 这类名词性内容。

senses.label: 尽管；不受……影响
senses.text: despite a difficulty or fact

usage_groups.group_title: 有阻碍或不利条件，结果仍然发生

examples.sentence: In spite of the rain, the lecture hall was full.
examples.translation: 尽管下雨，讲堂还是坐满了人。
examples.teaching_note: in spite of 后接名词短语 the rain。

examples.sentence: She finished the experiment in spite of repeated equipment failures.
examples.translation: 尽管设备反复故障，她还是完成了实验。
examples.teaching_note: 让步结构突出“阻碍存在，但结果仍发生”。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Despite the delay, the team completed the work.
basic_synonyms.translation: 尽管延误，团队还是完成了工作。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: despite 与 in spite of 基本等义，更简洁。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The policy continued, notwithstanding public criticism.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 尽管有公众批评，政策仍继续实施。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: notwithstanding 更正式。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Although it rained, the lecture hall was full.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 虽然下雨，讲堂还是坐满了人。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: although 后接完整从句；in spite of 后接名词性内容。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Because of the rain, the event was canceled.
confusable_words.translation: 因为下雨，活动被取消了。
confusable_words.difference_note: because of 表示原因；in spite of 表示让步。

extension.root_affix: 这是短语，不做词根拆解。重点记结构：in spite of + noun / -ing。

related_words.note: 最直接替换。
后接完整句子，结构不同。

source.raw_ocr_text: v.i. 退却; 后退; iB4% suddenly spring or flinch back in fear, horror, or disgust: The advancing troops recoiled before the counterattack. #E# i BPA MARR. | She recoiled in horror from the snake. 她看见蛇吓得直往后缩 。 £5444 shrink

headline.core_en: to suddenly move back in fear, disgust, or shock; a backward kick or reaction

familiar_bridge.summary: recoil 的画面是“突然往后缩”：人会因恐惧或厌恶后退，枪也会反冲。

core.main: recoil 可指身体突然后退，也可指心理上对某个想法感到厌恶而退缩；名词可指枪械反冲。

senses.label: 畏缩；后退
senses.text: to flinch or move back suddenly

senses.label: 反感；退缩
senses.text: to react with horror or disgust

senses.label: 反冲
senses.text: a backward kick from a gun or force

usage_groups.group_title: 身体因恐惧、厌恶或冲击而后退

examples.sentence: The child recoiled from the dead insect on the table.
examples.translation: 孩子看到桌上的死昆虫后往后一缩。
examples.teaching_note: recoil from 表示因害怕或厌恶退开。

examples.sentence: The advancing troops recoiled before the counterattack.
examples.translation: 进攻部队在反击面前退却。
examples.teaching_note: 接近表示军事上的后退。

usage_groups.group_title: 心理上对想法或行为感到反感

examples.sentence: Many readers recoil at the novel's casual cruelty.
examples.translation: 许多读者对小说中随意呈现的残酷感到反感。
examples.teaching_note: 心理上的 recoil 不是身体移动，而是强烈抗拒。

usage_groups.group_title: 枪械或机器的反冲
usage_groups.when_to_use: 用于 gun、rifle、weapon、spring 等物理语境。

examples.sentence: The rifle's heavy recoil made it difficult to aim again quickly.
examples.translation: 步枪强烈的反冲使人很难快速重新瞄准。
examples.teaching_note: 名词 recoil 是物理反冲。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She did not flinch when the glass broke.
basic_synonyms.translation: 玻璃碎裂时她没有退缩。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: flinch 常指瞬间身体反应；recoil 可身体也可心理。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: He shrank back from the angry dog.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 他从那条愤怒的狗面前退缩。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: shrink back 更口语。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The scene filled her with revulsion.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 这个场景让她强烈反感。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: revulsion 是名词，表达心理反感。

antonyms.example_sentence: The soldiers advanced rather than recoiled.
antonyms.translation: 士兵没有退却，而是前进。
antonyms.difference_note: advance 与身体/军事后退相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: She embraced the idea that others recoiled from.
antonyms.translation: 她接受了别人反感的想法。
antonyms.difference_note: embrace 对应心理上的接受。

extension.root_affix: re- 有“回、向后”的方向；coil 有卷曲、盘绕感。recoil 可记成“向后弹回/缩回”。

word_family.note: 帮助理解反弹回缩的画面。

related_words.note: 身体反应近义词。

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: recoil now includes physical, psychological, and mechanical senses with clear from/at structures.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 被低估的; 没有受到足够重视的 not duly appreciated: underappreciated artists 被低估的艺术家

headline.core_cn: 被低估的；未受到足够重视的
headline.core_en: not valued or recognized as much as deserved

familiar_bridge.summary: appreciate 是“欣赏、重视”；underappreciated 就是价值没有被充分看见。

core.main: underappreciated 常形容 artist、worker、role、factor、risk、book、skill，表示它真实价值高于人们给它的关注。

senses.label: 被低估；未受重视
senses.text: not properly valued or recognized

usage_groups.group_title: 人的贡献或事物价值没有得到应有认可

examples.sentence: The editor played an underappreciated role in shaping the book.
examples.translation: 编辑在塑造这本书的过程中发挥了被低估的作用。
examples.teaching_note: underappreciated role 表示贡献重要但未被充分承认。

examples.sentence: Sleep is an underappreciated factor in academic performance.
examples.translation: 睡眠是影响学业表现的一个被低估因素。
examples.teaching_note: 可用于分析中指出被忽视的重要因素。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The stock may be undervalued by the market.
basic_synonyms.translation: 这只股票可能被市场低估了。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: undervalued 常用于价值或价格；underappreciated 更强调认可不足。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The report highlights an overlooked cause of the crisis.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 报告强调了危机中一个被忽视的原因。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: overlooked 强调没被注意到；underappreciated 强调注意到了但评价不足。

advanced_synonyms.meaning_cn: 未被赞扬的；默默无闻的
advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The unheralded engineer solved the central problem.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 这位默默无闻的工程师解决了核心问题。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: unheralded 强调没有得到公开赞扬。

antonyms.example_sentence: The film is popular but overrated.
antonyms.translation: 这部电影很受欢迎，但被高估了。
antonyms.difference_note: overrated 与 underappreciated 评价方向相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: She became a celebrated novelist late in life.
antonyms.translation: 她晚年成为备受赞誉的小说家。
antonyms.difference_note: celebrated 强调获得广泛认可。

extension.root_affix: under- 表示不足；appreciate 表示欣赏、重视。underappreciated 就是“重视不够”。

word_family.note: 名词形式，较少见但清楚。

related_words.note: 帮助理解“被看见、被承认”的层面。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: underappreciated now explains recognition/value gap with clean under-/appreciate family.
revision_actions: Reviewed usage_tree for natural semantic grouping and student-facing clarity.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 人工制品; 手工艺品 an object made by a human being, typically an item of cultural or historical interest: artifacts stored in the rooms beneath the museum 储存在博物馆地下房间内的手工制品 n. (科研中 ) 人为造成的结果; 非自然的结果 something observed in a scientific investigation or experiment that is not naturally present but occurs as a result of the preparative or investigative procedure: Widespread tissue infection may be a technical artifact. 大规模的组织感染可能是实验方法造成的

headline.core_cn: 人工制品；文物；人为产物
headline.core_en: an object made by humans, especially one of historical or cultural interest; something produced by a process

familiar_bridge.summary: artifact 里的 art- 可联想到 human-made：不是自然物，而是人制造、留下或产生的东西。

core.main: artifact 最常指考古或文化中的人工制品，也可指实验、数据或程序中由操作过程造成的“人为产物”。

senses.label: 人工制品；文物
senses.text: a human-made object of cultural or historical interest

senses.label: 人为产物；假象
senses.text: something produced by a process rather than naturally present

usage_groups.group_title: 考古或文化中的人工制品

examples.sentence: The museum displayed artifacts from the ancient settlement.
examples.translation: 博物馆展出了来自古代定居点的文物。
examples.teaching_note: artifact 表示人制造并留下的物品。

usage_groups.group_title: 数据或图像中由过程造成的假象

examples.sentence: The apparent pattern was a statistical artifact, not a real trend.
examples.translation: 这个表面上的模式是统计假象，并不是真实趋势。
examples.teaching_note: 科学语境中 artifact 可指方法造成的假象。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The object was made of bronze.
basic_synonyms.translation: 这个物体由青铜制成。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: object 很宽泛；artifact 强调人造或过程产生。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The temple preserves relics from the early dynasty.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 寺庙保存了早期王朝的遗物。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: relic 更有历史或宗教遗留感。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The specimen was preserved for analysis.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 标本被保存以供分析。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: specimen 是供观察研究的样本，不一定人造。

antonyms.example_sentence: The stone was a natural object, not an artifact.
antonyms.translation: 这块石头是自然物，不是人工制品。
antonyms.difference_note: natural object 与 artifact 的人造性相对。

extension.root_affix: arti-/art 表示技艺、人为制作；fact 来自 make/do 的线索。artifact 可理解为“人做出来的东西”。

word_family.note: 同属人为制作/技巧语义场。
帮助理解 artifact 的人造性。
科学写作中指由方法或过程造成。

related_words.note: 与 artifact 的人造性相关。

source.raw_ocr_text: n. (Sts RAHA) 备忘录 a written message, especially in business: He sent a memo to the staff. 他给员工发了一份备忘录

headline.core_en: a short written message, especially within an organization

familiar_bridge.summary: memo 是 memorandum 的缩写，通常是公司或机构内部的简短书面通知。

core.main: memo 常用于 business、office、government、school 中，传达决定、提醒、政策或任务。比 letter 更内部、更简短。

senses.label: 备忘录；内部通知
senses.text: a short internal written message

usage_groups.group_title: 机构内部用短文传达事项
usage_groups.when_to_use: 用于 office、company、agency、school 等内部沟通。

examples.sentence: The director sent a memo explaining the new travel policy.
examples.translation: 主任发了一份备忘录，解释新的差旅政策。
examples.teaching_note: send a memo 是办公室语境常见表达。

examples.sentence: An internal memo warned that the project was over budget.
examples.translation: 一份内部备忘录警告该项目已经超预算。
examples.teaching_note: internal memo 强调组织内部文件。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She left a note on his desk.
basic_synonyms.translation: 她在他桌上留了一张便条。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: note 更普通；memo 通常是组织内部正式短文。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The agency released a memorandum on data security.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 该机构发布了关于数据安全的备忘录。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: memorandum 是 memo 的完整正式形式。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The school posted a notice about the schedule change.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 学校张贴了关于日程变化的通知。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: notice 可公开张贴；memo 多在内部流通。

extension.root_affix: memo 来自 memorandum，与 memory/memorial 有“记住、记录”的语义联系。

related_words.note: 帮助理解 memo 的功能。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: memo now has concise organizational-use treatment and memorandum/memory family links.
revision_actions: Reviewed usage_tree for natural semantic grouping and student-facing clarity.

source.raw_ocr_text: IER vt， (万指通过努力 ) 获得; 得到 succeed in obtaining (something), especially with difficulty: The division secured a major contract. 部门得到了一份大合同 。 3& 44 capture, earn

headline.core_en: to obtain or make certain; to fasten; safe and protected

familiar_bridge.summary: secure 作形容词是“安全的”；作动词可以是“确保安全”，也可表示经过努力获得。

core.main: secure 的动词义很重要：secure a contract / funding / victory 表示成功拿到；secure a door 表示固定、保护。形容词义是安全稳定。

senses.label: 获得；争取到
senses.text: to succeed in obtaining something

senses.label: 确保；保护；固定
senses.text: to make safe or fasten firmly

senses.label: 安全的；稳定的
senses.text: safe, protected, or confident

usage_groups.group_title: 经过努力拿到合同、资金或胜利

examples.sentence: The division secured a major contract after months of negotiation.
examples.translation: 经过数月谈判，该部门拿到了一份大合同。
examples.teaching_note: 接近原书例句，secure 表示成功获得。

examples.sentence: The researchers secured funding for a second trial.
examples.translation: 研究人员为第二次试验争取到了资金。
examples.teaching_note: secure funding 是学术和机构语境常见搭配。

usage_groups.group_title: 让地点、物品或系统安全固定

examples.sentence: Technicians secured the equipment before the storm arrived.
examples.translation: 技术人员在暴风雨到来前固定好了设备。
examples.teaching_note: secure equipment 指防止设备松动或受损。

usage_groups.group_title: 状态安全稳定，不容易失去

examples.sentence: A secure job allowed her to take care of her family.
examples.translation: 一份稳定的工作让她能够照顾家人。
examples.teaching_note: 形容词 secure 表示稳定、安全。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The team obtained permission to continue the study.
basic_synonyms.translation: 团队获得了继续研究的许可。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: obtain 是普通获得；secure 常暗示经过努力争取到。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The museum procured rare manuscripts from a private collection.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 博物馆从私人收藏中取得了珍贵手稿。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: procure 更正式，常指设法取得。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The rules safeguard patient privacy.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 这些规则保护病人隐私。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: safeguard 对应 secure 的保护义。

antonyms.example_sentence: The party lost the support it had secured earlier.
antonyms.translation: 该党失去了早先争取到的支持。
antonyms.difference_note: lose 与 secure 的获得相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: Weak passwords endanger secure data.
antonyms.translation: 弱密码会危及安全数据。
antonyms.difference_note: endanger 与 secure 的保护义相反。

extension.root_affix: secure 与 security 同族，核心是“安全、稳固”；动词义可从“使稳固”引申到“稳稳拿到”。

related_words.note: 帮助理解 secure a contract/funding 的正式动词义。

quality_gate.notes: Merged from a concrete ChatGPT deep-review result; pass requires clean canonical audit.

chatgpt_gate.llm_judgment: secure now handles obtain, protect/fasten, and safe adjective senses with source contract example preserved.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 猛烈批评的; 恶毒的仙 led with bitter criticism or malice: a vitriolic debate 言辞恶毒的争论近义词 acerbic

headline.core_cn: 尖刻恶毒的；充满刻薄批评的
headline.core_en: filled with bitter, harsh, or malicious criticism

familiar_bridge.summary: vitriolic 可以连到 acid：它像酸一样腐蚀人，形容语言特别尖刻恶毒。

core.main: vitriolic 常形容 attack、debate、criticism、comment、tone，强调言辞不仅严厉，而且带恶意和伤害性。

senses.label: 尖刻恶毒的
senses.text: bitterly harsh and malicious in language

usage_groups.group_title: 批评或争论言辞恶毒尖刻

examples.sentence: The policy debate became so vitriolic that compromise seemed impossible.
examples.translation: 政策辩论变得如此尖刻恶毒，以至于妥协似乎不可能。
examples.teaching_note: vitriolic debate 强调语言伤害性强。

examples.sentence: Her article drew vitriolic comments from opponents online.
examples.translation: 她的文章在网上招来了反对者的恶毒评论。
examples.teaching_note: 常用于现代公共讨论中的攻击性语言。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The debate turned bitter after the accusation.
basic_synonyms.translation: 指控之后，辩论变得尖酸。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: bitter 可指怨恨；vitriolic 更强，像语言攻击。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The critic's acerbic review angered the director.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 评论家尖酸的影评激怒了导演。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: acerbic 更像酸味刺人；vitriolic 更恶毒激烈。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: His caustic remarks embarrassed the committee.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 他刻薄的话让委员会难堪。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: caustic 也有腐蚀感，语气接近但可稍更讽刺。

antonyms.example_sentence: The two sides managed to keep the discussion civil.
antonyms.translation: 双方设法让讨论保持礼貌克制。
antonyms.difference_note: civil 与 vitriolic 的恶毒冲突相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: Her measured criticism avoided personal attacks.
antonyms.translation: 她克制审慎的批评避免了人身攻击。
antonyms.difference_note: measured 强调语言有节制。

extension.root_affix: vitriol 原指强酸，后来引申为尖刻恶毒的话；vitriolic 就是“像酸一样伤人的”。

word_family.meaning_cn: 尖刻恶毒的话；硫酸类强酸

related_words.note: 帮助理解语言像酸一样刺人。

source.raw_ocr_text: 3474) ¢X v.t. WAL; 实现 (功能等 ); 对 … 有用 be of use in achieving or satisfying: This book will serve a useful purpose. 这本书会有用处 。 v.i. 起 … 的作用 be of some specified use: The island's one pub serves as a cafe by day. % Eth #8 EA Ae ee

headline.core_cn: 服务；起作用；满足需要；担任
headline.core_en: to work for, be useful for, or fulfill a function

familiar_bridge.summary: serve 最熟的是“服务”；在学术语境里常表示“起到某种作用、满足某个目的”。

core.main: serve 的 GRE 相关义是 serve as / serve to / serve a purpose：某物承担功能、提供用途或帮助实现目标。

senses.label: 服务；供职
senses.text: to work for someone or an institution

senses.label: 起作用；满足目的
senses.text: to fulfill a function or purpose

senses.label: 提供食物或服务
senses.text: to provide food or help

usage_groups.group_title: 某物起到某种功能或用途
usage_groups.when_to_use: 用于 argument、example、policy、ritual、institution、evidence 等承担功能的对象。

examples.sentence: The example serves to clarify the author's main distinction.
examples.translation: 这个例子起到了澄清作者主要区分的作用。
examples.teaching_note: serve to do 表示起到某种说明作用。

examples.sentence: The old building now serves as a community center.
examples.translation: 这栋老楼现在用作社区中心。
examples.teaching_note: serve as 表示“充当、用作”。

usage_groups.group_title: 人在机构、岗位或群体中服务

examples.sentence: She served on the committee for three years.
examples.translation: 她在委员会任职三年。
examples.teaching_note: serve on 表示在委员会等机构中任职。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The diagram helps explain the process.
basic_synonyms.translation: 图表帮助解释这个过程。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: help 更日常；serve 常强调承担功能。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The rule functions as a safeguard.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 这条规则起到保障作用。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: function as 与 serve as 接近。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The program fulfills a basic need.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 这个项目满足了一项基本需求。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: fulfill 强调满足要求或目的。

antonyms.example_sentence: The rule hindered rather than served public interests.
antonyms.translation: 这条规则阻碍而不是服务公共利益。
antonyms.difference_note: hinder 与 serve a purpose 的促进作用相反。

extension.root_affix: serve 与 service、servant、servile 同族，核心是“服务、服从、发挥作用”。

word_family.note: GRE 常见相关词，带贬义。

related_words.note: 帮助理解 serve as / serve to 的学术用法。

source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. 488; 指责 formally accuse of or charge with a serious crime: J indict those citizens whose easy consciences condone such wrongdoings. 我批评那些宽容这类不道德行为而不受良心遗责的公民 。 近义词 denounce, excoriate 3

headline.core_en: to formally accuse someone of a serious crime; to criticize as blameworthy

familiar_bridge.summary: indict 的 c 不发音，法律义是“正式起诉”；引申义是“把某制度或行为当成罪证一样批判”。

core.main: indict 常用于 legal indictment，也常用于文章中说某事实 indicts a system/policy，表示构成严厉控诉。

senses.label: 正式起诉
senses.text: to formally charge with a crime

senses.label: 严厉指责；控诉
senses.text: to show that something is seriously wrong

usage_groups.group_title: 法律上正式起诉某人

examples.sentence: The former official was indicted for fraud.
examples.translation: 这名前官员因欺诈被起诉。
examples.teaching_note: be indicted for 是核心法律结构。

usage_groups.group_title: 事实或作品构成对制度的严厉控诉
usage_groups.when_to_use: 用于 evidence、report、novel、study、failure 等批判社会制度或行为。

examples.sentence: The report indicts the agency for ignoring repeated warnings.
examples.translation: 这份报告严厉指责该机构无视反复警告。
examples.teaching_note: 非法律语境中 indict 仍保留“正式控诉”的力度。

examples.sentence: The novel is an indictment of a society that values wealth over honesty.
examples.translation: 这部小说是对一个重财富轻诚实社会的控诉。
examples.teaching_note: indictment of 是常见名词搭配。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: They accused him of lying.
basic_synonyms.translation: 他们指责他说谎。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: accuse 更普通；indict 在法律上更正式，批评义更强。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: She was charged with theft.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 她被控盗窃。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: charge 是法律常用词；indict 通常指正式提起重罪指控。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The editorial condemned the decision as unjust.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 社论谴责该决定不公。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: condemn 是道德谴责，不一定有法律义。

antonyms.example_sentence: New evidence exonerated the suspect.
antonyms.translation: 新证据证明嫌疑人无罪。
antonyms.difference_note: exonerate 与 indict 的指控相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The jury acquitted the defendant.
antonyms.translation: 陪审团宣判被告无罪。
antonyms.difference_note: acquit 是法律判无罪。

extension.root_affix: indict 的 c 不发音；和 indictment 同族。重点记法律正式指控和强烈批判。

word_family.note: 名词形式，GRE 阅读常见。

related_words.note: 帮助理解 indict 的基本方向。

source.raw_ocr_text: #474) X vit. 使相容; 使一致 ， 使协调 to bring into agreement or concord; reconcile: accommodate a statement to facts 使言论符合事实 vt. 满足 … 的需求; 迎合 … 的需要 fit in with the wishes or needs of: He would ofien change his schedule to accommodate his clients. 他经常改变自己的安排来迁就客户 。 | accommodate the special needs of elderly residents 满足老年居民的特殊需求 v.i. (accommodate to) 适应 adapt to: accommodate to changed circumstances 适应改变的情势派生 accommodating /akpmaedertm/ adj. 与人方便的; 乐于助人的 fitting in with someone's wishes or demands in a helpful way: They are very accommodating to foreign visitors. 他们对外国游客很通融

headline.core_cn: 容纳；适应；满足需要；提供住宿
headline.core_en: to provide room for, adjust to, or meet someone's needs

familiar_bridge.summary: accommodate 可以连到 accommodate a guest：给人空间；抽象上就是给需求、差异或变化留出空间。

core.main: accommodate 有三条常见线索：容纳人或物；调整以适应需求；提供住宿。GRE 中常见 accommodate differences/needs。

senses.label: 容纳
senses.text: to have room for

senses.label: 适应；满足需求
senses.text: to adjust to someone's needs or wishes

senses.label: 提供住宿
senses.text: to provide lodging

usage_groups.group_title: 空间或系统能容纳更多人或事物
usage_groups.when_to_use: 用于 room、building、system、schedule、capacity 等。

examples.sentence: The new hall can accommodate five hundred people.
examples.translation: 新大厅可以容纳五百人。
examples.teaching_note: accommodate 指空间或容量装得下。

usage_groups.group_title: 调整安排以满足需求或差异

examples.sentence: The course was redesigned to accommodate students with different backgrounds.
examples.translation: 课程被重新设计，以适应背景不同的学生。
examples.teaching_note: 这里 accommodate 是“调整以照顾差异”。

examples.sentence: The committee tried to accommodate both scientific concerns and public fears.
examples.translation: 委员会试图兼顾科学关切和公众担忧。
examples.teaching_note: 正式语境中常表示平衡不同需求。

usage_groups.group_title: 为人提供住宿
usage_groups.when_to_use: 用于 hotel、hostel、family、university housing 等。

examples.sentence: The university could not accommodate all first-year students on campus.
examples.translation: 大学无法为所有一年级学生提供校内住宿。
examples.teaching_note: accommodate 在住宿语境中很常见。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The room fits twenty people comfortably.
basic_synonyms.translation: 这个房间可以舒适地容纳二十人。
basic_synonyms.difference_note: fit 更日常；accommodate 更正式，且可指照顾需求。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The program adapted to local conditions.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 项目适应了当地条件。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: adapt 强调自身改变；accommodate 强调为他人或条件留出空间。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The staff obliged by opening the room early.
advanced_synonyms.translation: 工作人员帮忙提前打开了房间。
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: oblige 可指满足请求，语气更偏人情。

antonyms.example_sentence: The policy excluded students with disabilities.
antonyms.translation: 这项政策排除了残障学生。
antonyms.difference_note: exclude 与 accommodate needs 的包容相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The office refused to accommodate the request.
antonyms.translation: 办公室拒绝满足该请求。
antonyms.difference_note: refuse 是不接受请求。

extension.root_affix: accommodate 与 accommodation 同族，核心是“给空间、作调整”。

word_family.meaning_cn: 乐于助人的；愿意配合的
word_family.note: 形容人愿意照顾他人需求。
正式形容词，常见于 policy。

related_words.note: 与 accommodate 的调整义相关。
